description: rekal deterministic memory: inject real recall on session start + each turn, auto-persist before compaction and at session end, and redirect file-based memory to rekal

hooks.prompt: Context is about to be compacted — persist durable knowledge to rekal NOW so nothing is lost. Procedure (the rekal-save skill has the full version if available): 1) Review the transcript and extract durable candidates: preferences with reasons, architecture/conventions, decisions and why, procedures, non-obvious bug root causes, behavior corrections. Skip transient state, trivially re-discoverable facts, vague platitudes, and secrets/keys/tokens (never store secrets). 2) Distill and compress each to a self-contained 1-2 sentence fact. 3) For each, call mcp__rekal__memory_search first; if the same topic already exists call mcp__rekal__memory_supersede, otherwise mcp__rekal__memory_store with a memory_type and 2-4 specific tags. Two memories about one topic must never coexist. 4) Produce NO user-facing output. Transcript: $ARGUMENTS

hooks.statusMessage: rekal: persisting memory at session end
hooks.prompt: The session is ending — persist durable knowledge to rekal NOW so nothing is lost. Procedure (the rekal-save skill has the full version if available): 1) Review the transcript and extract durable candidates: preferences with reasons, architecture/conventions, decisions and why, procedures, non-obvious bug root causes, behavior corrections. Skip transient state, trivially re-discoverable facts, vague platitudes, and secrets/keys/tokens (never store secrets). 2) Distill and compress each to a self-contained 1-2 sentence fact. 3) For each, call mcp__rekal__memory_search first; if the same topic already exists call mcp__rekal__memory_supersede, otherwise mcp__rekal__memory_store with a memory_type and 2-4 specific tags. Two memories about one topic must never coexist. 4) Produce NO user-facing output. Transcript: $ARGUMENTS
